name: Entrebank
description_short: EntreBank is a small business bank in Bloomington, MN offering commercial lending, business accounts, and treasury management with relationship-driven service.
description_long:
EntreBank positions itself as a community-focused small business bank headquartered in Bloomington, Minnesota, serving entrepreneurs and business owners across various industries. The bank emphasizes a relationship-first approach to commercial banking, contrasting itself with larger institutions by claiming their bankers "listen longer, dig deeper, and respond faster" to understand client needs.

The company offers a comprehensive suite of business banking services including business checking accounts, trust accounts, money market accounts, and various lending products. On the lending side, EntreBank provides financing for real estate purchases, equipment acquisition, working capital needs, and other business capital requirements. They also offer Treasury Management services featuring real-time account access, enhanced security features, and electronic payment capabilities including mobile deposit and bill pay.

EntreBank differentiates itself through three main pillars: creative problem-solving in lending (emphasizing "out of the box" solutions), modern digital banking technology (state-of-the-art platform with QuickBooks integration, real-time alerts, and multi-device access), and experienced relationship banking. Client testimonials highlight bankers by name (Todd, Melissa) and emphasize personalized attention, responsiveness, and willingness to finance during difficult market conditions when competitors were pulling back.

As a regional bank, EntreBank's primary limitation is geographic scope—service appears concentrated in the Bloomington, Minnesota area with no mention of multi-state operations. Pricing details, specific loan terms, APR ranges, and minimum account balances are not disclosed on the website. The company targets established businesses and entrepreneurs rather than startups with no revenue, and does not appear to offer SBA loans or other guaranteed lending programs despite being categorized as a business lender.
pros: State-of-the-art digital banking platform with QuickBooks Direct Connect integration and multi-device compatibility
Named relationship bankers (Todd, Melissa) with decades of commercial banking experience willing to finance when competitors are retreating
Specific lending flexibility for real estate, equipment, working capital, and custom financing scenarios
Real-time account access with 24/7 monitoring capabilities including transaction alerts and mobile deposit
Demonstrated willingness to work with existing loan portfolios and refinance debt solutions
Treasury Management services with electronic data interchange (EDI) notifications and person-to-person payments
cons: No pricing information provided—no disclosure of APR ranges, loan terms, fees, or account minimums
Geographically limited to Bloomington, Minnesota area with no evidence of multi-state service
No mention of SBA loan programs, lines of credit, or merchant cash advance products despite broad business lending claims
Relationship-based model may require direct contact with specific bankers rather than streamlined online application
Testimonials are anecdotal; no third-party ratings, complaint data, or regulatory oversight information provided
best_for: Established small businesses in Minnesota requiring creative real estate or equipment financing solutions
Business owners who value relationship banking and personalized attention over quick online approvals
Companies seeking to refinance existing debt or restructure their current banking relationships
Entrepreneurs in industries (real estate, energy, construction) where alternative financing approaches are advantageous
diagnosis: EntreBank is best for established small to mid-sized businesses in the Minnesota area seeking relationship-driven commercial lending with creative financing solutions and modern digital banking capabilities. The primary caveat is lack of transparency on pricing and terms, combined with geographic limitation to Bloomington, Minnesota—requiring direct banker outreach rather than transparent online application and approval processes.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
